title: Watch: Body cam shows Patriots party at downtown Boston restaurant
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Boston police released body cam footage of Patriots players' private party at Estella restaurant after their AFC win.
Violations noted included smoking indoors, unauthorized adult entertainment, and after-hours drinking at the January party.
Estella owner Helder George Brandao received a three-day license suspension; no Patriots players were charged.

title: 4 things to know about new Patriots safety Kevin Byard
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots sign safety Kevin Byard to a one-year, $9 million deal after his All-Pro season with the Bears.
Byard, a former Titan, led the NFL with seven interceptions last season and boasts 36 career interceptions.
Byard, who played under Mike Vrabel in Tennessee, has never missed a game in his 10-year NFL career.

title: Man resentenced in 2011 slaying of Dorchester teen
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Nyasani Watt resentenced to 14-17 years for the 2011 Dorchester teen slaying, eligible for immediate parole.
Watt's initial 2013 murder conviction was overturned in 2024 due to denial of the right to counsel.
The 2011 shooting left 16-year-old Jaivon Blake dead and another teen, Kimoni Elliot, with life-long injuries.

title: Samuel Adams launches new ‘Our City. Our Beer.’ variety pack inspired by Boston sports stars
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Samuel Adams releases 'Our City. Our Beer.' variety pack with beers inspired by Boston sports stars.
The pack includes beers themed around Derrick White, Garrett Crochet, Julian Edelman, and Zdeno Chára.
A launch party is scheduled at Banners Kitchen & Tap in Boston on April 4 with athletes serving beers.

title: Boston Marathon race director to run with his 3 kids — and astronaut Sunita Williams
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Dave McGillivray will run his 54th Boston Marathon with his three children and retired astronaut Sunita Williams.
McGillivray, the Boston Marathon race director, began running the marathon in 1972 and has run it annually since 1973.
The 2026 race will honor McGillivray's ex-wife Susan Hurley, with family members fundraising for various charitable causes.

title: Jayson Tatum, Derrick White sound off on ‘ridiculous’ Jaylen Brown ejection
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown ejected before halftime during Celtics' loss to San Antonio after arguing a missed call.
Teammates Jayson Tatum and Derrick White criticized the second technical foul on Brown as unwarranted.
Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla supported Brown's reaction, and Brown hasn't commented publicly since ejection.

title: He’s running Boston to honor of his mother’s adventurous spirit
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: James Hebert, 62, from Encinitas, Calif., will run the 2026 Boston Marathon in honor of his late mother, Janice.
Janice Hebert, a former New Englander, enjoyed adventures like hang-gliding, skydiving, and ziplining throughout her life.
Hebert plans to wear a lilac-colored shirt in memory of his mother's adventurous spirit during the April 20 marathon.

title: Authorities look into possible vandalism of expensive geotubes on Nantucket
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Nantucket geotubes on Sconset Bluff damaged, suspected vandalism being investigated by former Boston Police Commissioner Ed Davis.
Sconset Beach Preservation Fund offers $10,000 reward for information leading to arrest related to geotubes vandalism.
Recent winter storm further damaged geotubes, threatening erosion protection and project compliance with Nantucket's Conservation Commission.

title: Immigration officials ordered to release Marlborough 14-year-old detained apparently as ‘hostage’ to target her father, Rep. Trahan says
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A federal judge ordered the release of a 14-year-old detained by immigration officials in Marlborough, Massachusetts.
Rep. Lori Trahan claims the detention was to coerce her father, while DHS says she was rescued from gang members.
ICE claims the girl was with suspected gang members from Brazil during an attempted home invasion investigation.

title: Mass. woman among victims in Utah triple homicide; fundraiser launched for husband
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Natalie Graves, an Easthampton resident, was killed in Utah during a multi-state triple homicide while hiking with her aunt.
The suspect allegedly killed another woman earlier before stealing a vehicle and heading to the trail where Graves was hiking.
A GoFundMe for Taylor Graves, husband of victim Natalie Graves, has raised over $38,000, nearing its financial goal.
